title: Designing High-Throughput Spring APIs for Production
excerpt: How I design Java and Spring Boot services for predictable latency under high request volume.
body: At scale, API performance is mostly about data-access discipline and consistency in service contracts. I focus on reducing expensive query paths before attempting micro-optimizations.
My approach combines targeted caching, indexing strategy, and API shape simplification so latency remains predictable as request volume grows.
I treat observability as part of design: every critical endpoint needs metrics, alerts, and dashboards from the first production rollout.

title: Touring Kodagu on Two Wheels
excerpt: A three-day cycling tour through the misty hills and coffee estates of Kodagu district.
body: The terrain of Kodagu challenges every cyclist, but the endless stretches of green coffee plantations make the uphill grinds worth it.
Over three days, we covered steep inclines, navigated monsoon-washed trails, and found some of the best local filter coffee at small roadside stops.
If you're planning a cycling trip here, hydration and a solid set of climbing gears are non-negotiable.

title: Event-Driven Workflows with Kafka and RabbitMQ
excerpt: Practical lessons from building asynchronous microservice workflows with mixed messaging patterns.
body: Kafka works well for high-throughput event streams, while RabbitMQ can simplify routing and task distribution for work queues. The key is using each where it fits best.
I design events as stable contracts and make consumers idempotent by default. This keeps retries safe and prevents cascading failures.
For production systems, dead-letter handling and message observability are mandatory, not optional.

title: Chasing the Monsoon in Malnad
excerpt: A photographic exploration of the Western Ghats during the peak monsoon season.
body: Shooting in the rain requires patience, weather-sealing, and an eye for the dramatic shifts in light. The Malnad region transforms completely under heavy rain.
I focused on macro shots of the local flora and the vast, overcast landscapes that give the hills their characteristic haunting beauty.
Getting the shot often meant waiting out intense downpours under whatever shelter we could find, but the atmosphere captured in the images is unparalleled.
